title: Chapter 6: The Wolf Who Wasn't Supposed to Exist
content: The pack was afraid of her.

Sera felt it the moment she walked into the great hall the morning after the hunt. The whispers started before she'd taken three steps — not the cruel, dismissive whispers she was used to, but something worse. Something edged with fear.
"Did you hear? She transformed."
"A *silver* wolf. I saw it with my own eyes."
"That's not natural. That's not right."
"Moon-Marked. She has to be Moon-Marked. My grandmother used to say — "
The last speaker cut off abruptly as Sera passed. The wolf — a young warrior she'd seen in the training yards — dropped his gaze to the floor like she might curse him just by looking at him.
She wasn't a pathetic wolf-less maid anymore.
She was something much more dangerous: an unknown.

Kael was already at the high table, flanked by Darius and the other senior wolves. His face was its usual mask of cold authority, but Sera didn't need the mind-link to notice the shadows under his eyes. He hadn't slept. After carrying her back to the castle, after depositing her in the healer's care, after standing guard outside her door for three hours (she'd counted through the mind-link's running commentary about moonlight-scent and silver-fur and *she transformed for us, Fenris, she actually transformed*), he'd finally been dragged to his own quarters by a thoroughly exasperated Darius.

*"She's here. She's walking. She looks okay. No — she looks BETTER than okay. She looks... radiant. Is that a wolf thing? Do awakened wolves glow? She's glowing. I'm pretty sure she's glowing. Can everyone else see the glowing or is it just me?! FOCUS, FENRIS. Pack meeting. Serious faces. No poetry."*
Sera took her position against the wall — a servant's place, even now — and tried to ignore the hundred pairs of eyes tracking her every movement.
"The hunt was successful," Kael announced, his voice carrying through the hall. "We found the rogue threat and eliminated it."
A murmur of approval rippled through the assembled wolves.

"However." The word cut through the approval like a knife. "The rogue was not alone. Our scouts have confirmed at least three more similar creatures in the eastern territories. Something is driving them into our lands. Something we have not yet identified."

*"And by 'something,' I mean 'probably my uncle Aldric using dark magic that should have died out three generations ago, but I can't SAY that because I have no PROOF and the nobles would call it a vendetta.' Politics. I HATE politics."*
Isolde rose from her seat among the nobles. "My Alpha, if I may..."
Kael's golden gaze shifted to her. "Speak."

"The rogue was defeated by... unexpected means." Isolde's voice was honey-sweet, her eyes sliding toward Sera with practiced concern. "Many of us witnessed the maid's transformation. A silver wolf. A *moonlight* wolf. This is not a normal awakening."
"The nature of her wolf is not your concern."

"With respect, my Alpha, it is *everyone's* concern." Isolde turned to address the hall, her voice rising with theatrical urgency. "The legends are clear. The Moon-Marked bring chaos. They disrupt the natural order. In the old days, wolves born under the crescent moon were put down at birth — not out of cruelty, but out of *necessity*. They were too dangerous to live!"
The murmurs grew louder. Angrier.
Sera's hands clenched at her sides. She could feel the creature in her chest stirring, responding to her fear with a protective growl.
*Easy*, she thought at it. *Not yet.*

*"She's calling for Sera's DEATH. In front of the ENTIRE PACK. With WORDS that sound REASONABLE. I'm going to — Kael, if you don't shut this down RIGHT NOW, I will find a way to take control of our body and I will transform and I will EAT her. Not kill. EAT. Slowly. Starting with her TONGUE."*
"Lady Vane."
Kael's voice was soft. Danger-soft.
"You speak of the old days. Of putting down children. Of legends and necessity." He rose from his seat, and the hall went absolutely silent. "But you forget one crucial detail."
Isolde's confidence flickered. "My Alpha?"
"The old days are *over*."
Kael descended from the dais, his footsteps echoing on the stone floor. The crowd parted before him like water before a blade.

"In the old days, we drowned Moon-Marked children. In the old days, we exiled anyone who questioned the Alpha's word. In the old days, we were *savages*." He stopped in the center of the hall, his golden eyes sweeping the assembled wolves. "I have spent my entire rule dragging this pack out of the old days. I will not be dragged back."
He turned to face Isolde directly.

"You will not speak of executing pack members in my hall again. You will not spread rumors designed to sow fear. And you will remember — " his voice dropped to something barely above a growl — "that the wolf you're calling an abomination *saved your life* three days ago. Every wolf in this room who is still breathing owes that breath to the 'Moon-Marked' girl you want to put down."
The silence that followed was absolute.

*"THAT'S MY BOY. That's my — I mean, that's ME. THAT'S US. We're KILLING it right now. Look at Isolde's face. LOOK AT IT. Is she turning purple? I think she's turning purple. This is the best council meeting we've ever had."*
Isolde's face was not purple, but it was a very interesting shade of red. She sat down without another word, her perfect composure finally cracked.

Kael returned to his seat. "The matter is closed. We face a real threat — rogue wolves in our territory, possibly connected to my exiled uncle's conspiracy. I will not waste time on superstition." His eyes found Sera's across the hall. "Seraphina Vale will begin training with Elder Magnus to control her transformation. Any wolf who interferes with that training will answer to me personally."

*"Training. Together. Well, not together-together. She'll train with Magnus. But we can WATCH the training. Casually. From a distance. Like a normal Alpha who is totally not obsessed with his maid's wolf form. Perfectly normal. Perfectly casual. I wonder what she looks like when she's concentrating. Probably adorable. She's probably adorable when she's concentrating."*
Sera pressed her lips together to contain the smile.
She was being trained. Officially. By the pack's lorekeeper.
Isolde had tried to have her executed and instead had accidentally gotten her legitimized.
The Moon Goddess had a sense of humor.

Elder Magnus's tower smelled of old parchment, dried herbs, and something that might have been wolf hair. The circular room at the top was crammed with bookshelves, scrolls, and artifacts that predated the pack's recorded history.
Sera sat cross-legged on a worn rug in the center of the room, her eyes closed, trying to do what Magnus was asking.
"Find it," the old wolf said, his voice a gentle rumble. "Your wolf has woken once. It will wake again. You simply need to learn how to call it."
"It's not that simple."
"Isn't it?"
Sera opened her eyes. "I've spent twenty-two years believing I was wolf-less. I've built my entire identity around being *broken*. You can't just tell me to — to *unbreak* myself in an afternoon."
Magnus settled onto a cushion across from her. "Tell me about the first time. The hunt. What were you feeling?"
*Terrified*, she almost said. *Helpless. Desperate.*
But that wasn't quite right, was it? She'd been all those things, yes — but there had been something else. Something underneath the fear.
"Angry," she said slowly. "I was angry. That thing was going to kill me, and I was going to die a wolf-less maid, and I was *angry* about it. Angry that I never got to — " She stopped.
"Never got to what?"
*Never got to tell him. Never got to kiss him properly. Never got to see if the mess inside his head and the mess inside mine could fit together into something that made sense.*
"Never got to find out who I really was," she said instead.

Magnus nodded slowly. "Your wolf responded to your anger. Your will to live. Your refusal to accept an unjust death." He leaned forward. "But it also responded to something else. Something that triggered the very first crack in your seal — three days ago, in the Alpha's study."
Sera's heart skipped. "How do you know about that?"
"I am old. I pay attention." Magnus's pale eyes were knowing. "You felt the seal crack during the assassination attempt. When Kael's fingers moved. When his eyes found yours. When he said — "
"*Mine*," Sera whispered.
The word hung in the air between them.

"Your wolf is tied to him," Magnus said quietly. "It has been since the moment the Moon Goddess created the link between you. His wolf and yours are... counterparts. Mirrors. Two halves of something that was broken long ago and is only now beginning to heal."
"That's why the Goddess chose me? Because our wolves match?"
"That is part of it. The rest..." Magnus rose, moving to a dusty bookshelf. He pulled down a scroll so old the edges crumbled at his touch. "...the rest is in here. The prophecy of the Silver Queen."
He unrolled the scroll. The script was ancient, barely legible, but Sera could make out a few words:
*...born under the dying moon, marked by the crescent light...*
*...she who hears the unspoken shall bridge the divide...*
*...the Silver Queen and the Alpha of Two Hearts shall unite the pack against the darkness that comes...*

"There's more," Magnus said. "Much more. But this is enough for today." He re-rolled the scroll with careful hands. "Your training, Seraphina, will not be about force. It will be about *feeling*. Your wolf responds to emotion — specifically, it responds to your connection with Kael. The stronger that connection grows, the stronger your wolf becomes."
"So I have to..." Sera's face heated. "...get closer to him?"
Magnus's beard twitched. "That is one interpretation."
"Elder Magnus, are you telling me that the key to unlocking my wolf powers is *romance*?"
"I am telling you that the Moon Goddess works in mysterious ways." The old wolf's eyes were definitely twinkling now. "Some ways more mysterious than others."
Sera dropped her head into her hands.
The Moon Goddess was absolutely, definitely, one hundred percent laughing at her.
